description_long: This object is a decorative medal featuring a cross-shaped metallic pendant with blue enamel arms and a central circular emblem, likely representing an insignia or coat of arms. The pendant is topped with an ornate crown-shaped element, connecting it to a folded red ribbon with vertical blue stripes. The ribbon is pleated and secured with a small metallic fastener, and it bears a handwritten marking in black ink. The central medallion is surrounded by a green laurel wreath motif, and the overall design is intricate, with detailed relief work on the metal surfaces. The reverse side of the medal is less ornate, showing the plain back of the cross and the ribbon. The object is likely made of metal and fabric, with a combination of painted or enameled surfaces and textile elements.
description: A decorative medal with a blue and silver cross pendant, ornate crown, and a red-and-blue striped ribbon, featuring a central emblem and laurel motif. The ribbon is pleated and marked with handwritten numbers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed cross pendant, central emblem, ornate crown, and the pleated ribbon with visible handwritten numbers.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the medal and ribbon, with the layered construction of the metal and fabric components.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back of the cross pendant and the unmarked side of the ribbon.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the connection between the pendant and the ribbon.
